title: Role-based access control with RequireRole plug and role hierarchy

story.i_want_to: implement a RequireRole plug that enforces a role hierarchy (superadmin > user > orchestrator > agent) and rejects requests from API keys with insufficient role permissions
story.so_that: each API endpoint can declare its minimum required role and the system automatically prevents unauthorized access based on the two-tier trust model

rationale: The two-tier trust model is the core security property of loopctl: agents can only write agent_status, orchestrators can only write verified_status, and users manage configuration. The RequireRole plug enforces this at the router level. The role hierarchy means a superadmin can do anything a user can, a user can do anything an orchestrator can, etc. This prevents the need to list every allowed role on every endpoint.

acceptance_criteria.description: LoopctlWeb.Plugs.RequireRole accepts a :role option specifying the minimum required role level
The role hierarchy is: superadmin (level 4) > user (level 3) > orchestrator (level 2) > agent (level 1). Higher levels can access endpoints requiring lower levels.
RequireRole reads :current_api_key from conn.assigns and compares its role against the required minimum
If the API key's role is below the minimum, the plug halts the connection with 403 Forbidden and a JSON error body
If the API key's role meets or exceeds the minimum, the plug passes through without halting
An :exact_role option is available for endpoints that require exactly one role (e.g., agent-only endpoints like /stories/:id/claim)
A helper function role_at_least?/2 is available for use in controller actions for fine-grained role checks
The plug can be used in router pipelines or individual controller plugs: plug RequireRole, role: :user

test_cases.name: Agent cannot access user-level endpoint
expected_results: Response body is JSON with error.message indicating insufficient permissions

test_cases.name: Exact role check rejects higher roles
expected_results: Only :agent role keys can access this endpoint

test_cases.name: Full integration: agent key rejected from user endpoint

technical_notes: Role hierarchy as a map: %{superadmin: 4, user: 3, orchestrator: 2, agent: 1}
The exact_role option is needed for agent-only endpoints (claim, start, report) where orchestrators should NOT be able to act as agents
Consider implementing the role hierarchy in a separate Loopctl.Auth.Role module for reuse
The plug should work both as a pipeline plug and as an inline controller plug
Error message should indicate the required role without revealing the full hierarchy
